stations.note: Corrected from an unsourced 'town center' placeholder to the geocoded street address (1140 Gardner Rd., Westchester, IL 60154) given in MWRD's Contract 21-168-3S (Westchester Pumping Station Relief Sewer) SRF project notification. Built 1951; MWRD internal facility #150 ("WCPS").
stations.source: MWRD, "21-168-3S Westchester Pumping Station Relief Sewer, SSA" SRF Project Public Notification <https://mwrd.org/media/8726>; geocoded via Nominatim/OpenStreetMap.

connections.label: Westchester PS CSO Outfall 150 to Addison Creek
connections.source: MWRD CSO Representative Monitoring and Reporting Plans (rev. 2024): Outfall 150, TARP structure DS-D34-AI, receiving water Addison Creek, owner MWRD <https://mwrd.org/sites/default/files/2024-03/CSO_Representative_Monitoring_and_Reporting_Plans_OBrien,_Stickney,_Calumet_and_Lemont_WRPs.pdf>

connections.label: Berkley-Hillside Intercepting Sewer (relieved by Westchester PS)
connections.source: MWRD, "21-168-3S Westchester Pumping Station Relief Sewer, SSA" project justification <https://mwrd.org/media/8726>; Hillside, IL anchor point geocoded via Nominatim. Route is a straight approximation between Hillside and the pumping station -- exact sewer alignment not documented in sources reviewed.

connections.label: Broadview-Bellwood Intercepting/Outfall Sewer (relieved by Westchester PS)
connections.source: MWRD, Contract 21-168-3S SRF fact sheet <https://mwrd.org/media/8726>; Sanitary District of Chicago, Engineering Works (1928) describes the related Broadview-Bellwood Outfall Sewer and Des Plaines River Intercepting Sewer of that era (sources/sanitary-district-reports/1928-engineering-works-sanitary-district-of-chicago.pdf). Bellwood, IL anchor point geocoded via Nominatim. 'from' year (1920) is an approximate era inference from the 1928 report, not a directly documented construction date.

connections.label: 2023 relief sewer: Berkley-Hillside Interceptor to TARP Drop Shaft DS-D34-AI (Des Plaines Tunnel System, "Roosevelt Road leg")
connections.source: MWRD, Contract 21-168-3S (awarded Dec. 21, 2023, $6,775,766, contractor Rausch Infrastructure LLC): ~275 ft of 45-in. GRP pipe by microtunneling connecting the Berkley-Hillside Intercepting Sewer to TARP Drop Shaft DS-D34-AI <https://mwrd.org/media/8726>

connections.label: Addison Creek to Salt Creek confluence (Addison Creek is a Salt Creek tributary, not a direct Des Plaines River tributary)
connections.source: Wikipedia, "Addison Creek (Salt Creek tributary)"; USGS GNIS Feature ID 421693 (mouth ~41.9484,-87.9309) <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Addison_Creek_(Salt_Creek_tributary)>

connections.label: Kirie WRP effluent Outfall 001 to Higgins Creek
connections.source: Illinois EPA NPDES Permit No. IL0047741 (Kirie WRP), 2013 public-notice fact sheet, Outfall 001 at 42 deg 01'10"N, 87 deg 56'12"W <https://epa.illinois.gov/content/dam/soi/en/web/epa/documents/public-notices/2013/mwrdgc-kirie.pdf>

connections.label: Weller Creek to Des Plaines River confluence near Des Plaines, IL
connections.source: USGS monitoring location "Weller Creek at Des Plaines, IL" (USGS-05530000), 42.0495,-87.9181 <https://waterdata.usgs.gov/monitoring-location/USGS-05530000/>

connections.label: Upper Des Plaines Intercepting Sewer route (Elmhurst Rd. / Weller Creek / William St. corridor, intercepting Weller Creek and Feehanville Ditch CSOs)
connections.source: U.S. EPA Region V, Final EIS for the MSDGC Des Plaines-O'Hare WRP and Solids Pipeline, Vol. 1 (May 1975): tunnel along Elmhurst Rd. between the Northwest Tollway and Weller Creek, along Weller Creek between Elmhurst Rd. and Central Rd., branch from Elmhurst Rd./Lonnquist Blvd. east to William St. then north to Rand Rd./Isabella St. (sources/suburban-wrps/1975-epa-final-eis-ohare-wrp-solids-pipeline-vol1.pdf); street anchor points geocoded via Nominatim.

connections.label: Gloria Alitto Majewski Reservoir pumpback to Kirie WRP
connections.source: MWRD TARP Fact Sheet; docs/07 and docs/13 of this archive (Majewski Reservoir stores Upper Des Plaines TARP combined sewage, pumped back to Kirie for treatment when capacity available)

connections.label: Kirie-Egan Solids Pipeline (raw sludge, Kirie to Egan for digestion)
connections.source: TRACED from the 1974 MSDGC as-built engineering contract map (Figure 4-2, 1975 O'Hare WRP/solids-pipeline EIS): Kirie WRP south to Oakton St; west on Oakton to (S) Arlington Heights Rd, Elk Grove Village; south on Arlington Heights Rd; west via Cosman Rd and the old Rohlwing Rd/Route 53 corridor north to Egan WRP. Every named street cross-checked against modern OSM. Confirmed still in active service by a 2026 MWRD rehabilitation contract. Confidence HIGH; corners simplified.

connections.label: Egan WRP effluent to Upper Salt Creek
connections.source: Illinois EPA/MWRD NPDES Permit No. IL0036340 (Egan WRP), Outfalls A01/B01/001 to Salt Creek <https://mwrd.org/media/13261>. Exact outfall coordinates not given in the current permit text (unlike Kirie's 2013-vintage public-notice fact sheet); short line from plant coordinate is approximate.

connections.label: Salt Creek route (Schaumburg area past Elmhurst, Oak Brook, Brookfield) to the Des Plaines River near Riverside/Lyons
connections.source: USGS GNIS Salt Creek mouth coordinates (~41.8142,-87.8353) via Wikidata Q16984309; USACE Rivergages "Des Plaines River at Riverside, IL" gauge sited ~3,600 ft downstream of the Salt Creek confluence <https://rivergages.mvr.usace.army.mil/WaterControl/stationinfo2.cfm?sid=RVRI2>; intermediate town anchors (Elmhurst, Oak Brook, Brookfield) geocoded via Nominatim as a smoothed approximation of Salt Creek's actual meandering course.

connections.label: Egan digested biosolids to O'Brien WRP, en route to Stickney
connections.source: MWRD Egan WRP fact sheet; docs/13 of this archive. Exact pipeline/truck route not documented in sources reviewed; straight-line approximation along the I-90 corridor. || ROUTE MINING (2026): street-level route GENUINELY UNDOCUMENTED — full 158k-page FTS corpus, MWRD FY2021-26 capital/major-contracts books, and the 2026 Collection System O&M Manual (which defines 'sludge and centrate lines' as a category without listing routes) all searched; web search likewise empty. Stays symbolic.

connections.label: O'Brien → Stickney sludge line, Section 1 (McCormick Blvd → Devon Ave, documented)
connections.source: MWRD 'North Side Sludge Pipeline Section 1' (three documented phases): O'Brien WRP south along McCormick Blvd (paralleling the North Shore Channel) through Lincolnwood to Devon Ave, then west on Devon; McCormick/Devon and Devon/Cicero junctions verified via OSM shared-node queries. The Pulaski/Crawford-area end waypoint is less certain. Continues as the symbolic segment toward Stickney — remainder of the route undocumented.

connections.label: Hanover Park WRP effluent (Outfall 007) to West Branch DuPage River
connections.source: Illinois EPA/MWRD NPDES Permit No. IL0036137 (Hanover Park WRP), Discharge/Outfall 007 <https://mwrd.org/media/13266>. Exact outfall coordinates not given in the permit text reviewed; short line from plant coordinate is approximate.

connections.label: Hanover Park biosolids (subsurface injection) to adjacent Fischer Farm / Upper DuPage Reservoir
connections.source: MWRD Hanover Park WRP fact sheet; docs/13 of this archive (200-acre Fischer Farm purchased 1969, adjacent to the plant)

connections.label: Lemont WRP effluent to Chicago Sanitary & Ship Canal
connections.source: MWRD Lemont WRP fact sheet; docs/13 of this archive (plant sited directly on the CSSC at 13 Stephen St., Lemont)

connections.label: Lemont digested sludge trucked to Stickney (or Calumet) WRP for further processing/drying
connections.source: MWRD Lemont WRP fact sheet; docs/13 of this archive. Trucked (not piped); route is an illustrative straight-line approximation, not a documented haul route.

connections.label: McCook Main Tunnel — Mainstream Tunnel/PS ↔ reservoir (bidirectional)
connections.source: 33-ft finished dia., 1,600 ft, bored in Silurian dolomite from an ~90-ft-dia x ~300-ft gate/access shaft at its midpoint (six 14.5x29.5-ft high-head wheel gates). USACE/MWRD, Black & Veatch design, Kiewit construction; live Dec 2017 (McCook Stage 1). Trade sources state it accommodates both gravity inflow from the surcharging Mainstream Tunnel and the pumped return path to the Mainstream PS.

connections.label: Des Plaines Inflow Tunnel → McCook Reservoir (gravity)
connections.source: Separate ~20-ft-dia gravity inflow tunnel from the Des Plaines Tunnel System, with its own gate shaft (primary+backup gates, control building, portal, 'tiger-teeth' energy-dissipation apron). Black & Veatch/Walsh, awarded June 2016, substantial completion Oct 2021, $109.9M. Route schematic between documented endpoints.

connections.label: Mainstream PS dewatering → Stickney WRP (~4.5 mi; exact route undocumented)
connections.source: After storms the Mainstream Pumping Station's 8 pumps (largest 17,500 hp, ~250-300-ft lift) dewater the Mainstream/Des Plaines tunnels and McCook Reservoir to Stickney WRP for treatment, ~4.5 mi northeast. The trunk force-main/conduit's diameter and street route are genuinely undocumented in sources reviewed — drawn schematically.

connections.label: Calumet TARP system → Thornton Composite Reservoir (bidirectional)
connections.source: Final spur: a single 30-ft-I.D. concrete-lined connecting tunnel (~1,300 ft) from an existing Calumet-system stub, bifurcating through a four-gate wet-well shaft and recombining to the reservoir portal (KMI Architects/Engineers; gates ~100 tons each; live end of 2015). Southern corridor inferred to be the Little Calumet Leg (8.0 mi, 18.2-ft dia., bored 2003-04, operational ~2006, Jay Dee/Affholder JV) — no source explicitly maps its route, so this line is schematic. Gravity inflow; pumped return through the same corridor to the Calumet TARP PS and on to Calumet WRP.

connections.label: O'Brien → Stickney sludge line, remainder (route undocumented — symbolic)
connections.source: Continuation from the documented Devon Ave corridor (North Side Sludge Pipeline Section 1) to Stickney WRP for final dewatering/drying. No source documents the alignment south of the Devon corridor; drawn as a symbolic arc.
